data_in.description: MS2 spectra files in mzML, mzXML, or MGF format

data_out.description: Per-sample MS2 fingerprint vector (numeric array or matrix format)

domain_knowledge: MS2 fingerprinting counts the raw occurrence frequency of fragment m/z values and neutral losses (precursor m/z minus fragment m/z) rather than their intensity or abundance.
Neutral losses are calculated by subtracting each observed fragment m/z from the precursor ion m/z of the parent spectrum.
The fingerprint vector aggregates peak and neutral loss counts across all spectra belonging to a single sample, creating a sample-level rather than spectrum-level representation.
The MS2 fingerprint approach is retention-time agnostic, making it suitable for comparing samples acquired under different LC or instrument conditions without chromatographic alignment.

direct_checks: verify that the implementation accepts MS2 spectra files as input (file_format_is: common MS formats such as .mgf, .msp, or .mzML)
verify that the implementation produces a numeric fingerprint vector output for each sample (format_is: numeric array or matrix, e.g., numpy array, CSV, or HDF5)

verify that the fingerprint vector contains counts of MS2 peaks and neutral losses (field_present: vector entries represent peak/loss counts; robust to parameter choices for binning or filtering thresholds)
verify that output fingerprint dimensions match the number of input samples (row_count_equals: one fingerprint per sample)
script_runs: execute the MS2_FINGERPRINT_COMPONENT on a reference MS2 spectra dataset (e.g., from mandelbrot-project/memo_publication_examples) without errors

output_matches_reference: compare generated fingerprints against documented reference fingerprints (if available in the publication examples repository) byte-for-byte or within documented numerical tolerance
expert_review: assess whether peak and neutral loss counting methodology aligns with MS2 fragmentation mass spectrometry best practices and the MEMO publication intent
evaluate whether the fingerprint vector dimensionality and binning strategy are appropriate for downstream alignment and sample comparison tasks
review whether handling of precursor ions and neutral loss calculations is biochemically sound

cmd: import matchms; import numpy as np; from spec2vec import SpectrumDocument; from matchms.importing_utils import load_from_mgf; spectra = load_from_mgf('{inputs.ms2_spectra}'); fingerprints = []; sample_peaks = {}; sample_losses = {}; [sample_peaks.setdefault(s.metadata.get('sample_id', 'unknown'), {}).update({p[0]: sample_peaks.get(s.metadata.get('sample_id', 'unknown'), {}).get(p[0], 0) + 1 for p in s.peaks.intensity}) for s in spectra]; [sample_losses.setdefault(s.metadata.get('sample_id', 'unknown'), {}).update({s.metadata.get('precursor_mz', 0) - p[0]: sample_losses.get(s.metadata.get('sample_id', 'unknown'), {}).get(s.metadata.get('precursor_mz', 0) - p[0], 0) + 1 for p in s.peaks.intensity}) for s in spectra]; all_peaks = sorted(set(pk for sample in sample_peaks.values() for pk in sample.keys())); all_losses = sorted(set(ls for sample in sample_losses.values() for ls in sample.keys())); fingerprint_matrix = np.array([[sample_peaks.get(sid, {}).get(pk, 0) for pk in all_peaks] + [sample_losses.get(sid, {}).get(ls, 0) for ls in all_losses] for sid in sorted(set(list(sample_peaks.keys()) + list(sample_losses.keys())))]); np.savetxt('{task_id}_fingerprints.csv', fingerprint_matrix, delimiter=','); print('Fingerprint generation complete'); print(f'Fingerprint shape: {fingerprint_matrix.shape}')

expected_outputs: Per-sample MS2 fingerprint vector (numeric array or matrix format)
finding: MS2 fingerprints are generated by counting the occurrence of MS2 peaks and neutral losses (relative to the precursor ion) in each sample, producing a numeric fingerprint representation.
inputs: MS2 spectra files in mzML, mzXML, or MGF format
methodology_summary: Parse MS2 spectra files using matchms to extract precursor m/z and fragment peaks.
Count occurrences of all detected m/z peaks within each sample across all constituent spectra.
Calculate neutral losses by subtracting fragment m/z from precursor m/z for each peak.
Count occurrences of all unique neutral losses within each sample.
Aggregate and combine peak and neutral loss counts into a single numeric fingerprint vector per sample using spec2vec document representation.

Validation: fingerprint vectors must have non-zero counts for peaks and losses present in the sample and dimensionality consistent with the union of all observed peaks and losses across the sample set.

task_description: Count MS2 peaks and neutral losses across all spectra in a sample set to generate a per-sample MS2 fingerprint vector. Output one fingerprint vector per sample as a numeric representation suitable for downstream alignment and comparison.

task_inputs.label: MS2 spectra files in mzML, mzXML, or MGF format

task_objective: Implement the MS2 fingerprint generation operation that counts occurrences of MS2 peaks and neutral losses per sample to produce a numeric fingerprint vector.
title: Reconstruct the MS2 fingerprint generation step from raw spectra using MEMO
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Python 3.8, numpy.
workflow_description: 1. Load MS2 spectra files using matchms to parse fragmentation data. 2. Iterate through spectra in each sample and extract precursor m/z values. 3. Count occurrences of all MS2 peaks (m/z values above noise threshold) in each spectrum. 4. Calculate neutral losses by subtracting observed fragment m/z from precursor m/z for each peak. 5. Count occurrences of all unique neutral losses per sample. 6. Aggregate peak and neutral loss counts across all spectra within each sample using spec2vec document conversion. 7. Generate a numeric fingerprint vector per sample combining peak and neutral loss occurrence counts, normalized by sample size.
